topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, providing a curated collection of prompt examples for various AI chat models, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide with over 25 chapters that cover everything from basic to advanced prompting techniques, including chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the purpose of 'Prompting for Kids'?
entries.a: 'Prompting for Kids' is an interactive, game-based adventure designed to teach children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through fun puzzles and stories.

entries.q: How can I self-host my own prompt library?
entries.a: You can self-host by using the command 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' to create a new library, followed by 'cd my-prompt-library' to navigate into it. A setup wizard will help configure branding, themes, and authentication.

entries.q: What integrations are available with prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat offers integrations such as a command-line interface (CLI) for easy access, a Claude Code Plugin for enhanced functionality, and the ability to use it as an MCP server in AI tools.

entries.q: What are some advanced prompting techniques covered in the Interactive Book?
entries.a: The Interactive Book covers advanced techniques such as chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning, which help improve the effectiveness of AI interactions.
